name: Tex-Mex Biscuit Sandwiches
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 ounces deli roast beef, chopped (1/2 cup)
10 ounce can Hungry Jack refrigerated rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. In a medium bowl, combine roast beef, taco sauce, barbecue sauce, 1/4 cup each of ripe and green olives, green onions, and cheese. Set aside.
3. Separate the dough into 10 biscuits. Dip both sides in cornmeal.
4. Press on one side of each biscuit to form a 5-inch circle. Place 5 circles on an ungreased cookie sheet.
5. Top each with 1/4 cup of beef mixture. Brush edges lightly with water.
6. Place the remaining biscuit circles over the top of the beef mixture. Press edges together with a fork to seal.
7. Using a tablespoon, make an indentation in the center of each sandwich. Sprinkle the top with the remaining cornmeal.
8. Bake at 350°F for 14-22 minutes or until golden brown. Remove from oven; gently repeat indentation if necessary.
9. Fill each indentation with a heaping tablespoonful of sour cream. Garnish each with 2 pimiento slices and two ripe olive slices, if desired.
Makes 5 sandwiches.
Per sandwich: 340 calories, 12 g protein, 31 g carbohydrate, 19 g fat, 35 mg cholesterol, 950 mg sodium
Pillsbury Classic Cookbook #86, 33rd Bake-Off, p. 35
